response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems software developer with over 14 years of experience, but his background is centered on web platforms, product engineering, and application-level tooling. The role demands SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his history. His low-latency DLang work and C/C++ stint at National Instruments show systems aptitude, but that is far from the silicon-level performance architecture expertise required at principal level.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems programming experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers, services handling millions of requests per second)
14+ years of professional experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years educational path
Broad technical versatility across languages and platforms, including C/C++ and embedded-adjacent work
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience of any kind
No deep learning training/inference optimization, GPU workload analysis, or AI accelerator exposure
Career trajectory is web and full-stack product development, misaligned with a principal-level hardware performance role

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design expertise
No AI accelerator or GPU performance background
